paragraphs.text:
FerroFlux nodes are configured through .flux files — a strict,
      strongly-typed configuration DSL that compiles to an immutable runtime
      manifest at boot. This page documents the four top-level blocks, shows
      annotated examples, and provides a searchable reference of all 47
      configuration keys.

The global block defines node identity, logging behavior, and
      runtime limits. Exactly one global block is required per
      configuration tree; subsequent definitions raise E_DUP_GLOBAL at parse time.

Identity fields (node_id, region) become immutable
      once the node has registered with a broker — changing them requires a full
      re-pairing. Most other keys hot-reload on SIGHUP.

A broker block declares a connection to an upstream FerroFlux
      Gateway (or compatible MQTT 5 / AMQP 1.0 endpoint). You may declare
      multiple profiles; the runtime fails over in declaration order when the
      primary becomes unreachable for more than failover_grace seconds.

Device schemas describe the shape of telemetry payloads that a sensor
      class is expected to publish. The compiler turns each schema
      block into a zero-copy validator backed by serde + prost;
      payloads that fail validation are dropped and surface as
      schema_violations_total in the metrics endpoint.

Field types: f32, f64, i32, i64,
      u32, u64, bool, string(N),
      enum(...), and the composite vec3 for accelerometer triples.

Retention policies bind to streams (matched by glob) and control how long
      raw and downsampled data remain on disk before being rolled up or evicted.
      A node may define any number of policies; the first matching glob wins.

The table below enumerates all 47 configuration keys recognized by the
      v2.4 parser. Use the filters to narrow by block or required-status.
      Deprecated keys are flagged and will emit a warning at parse time.

items.text: global.node_idstring—yesUnique node identifier within the tenant.
global.log_levelenuminfonoLogging verbosity: trace, debug, info, warn, error.
global.allow_plaintext DEPRECATEDboolfalsenoAllowed unencrypted broker links; removed in v3.0.
global.shutdown_graceduration"15s"noTime given to drain queues at shutdown.
broker.endpointurl—yesBroker URL with scheme and port.
broker.tenantstring—yesTenant slug provisioned on the gateway.
broker.secret_refuri—yesVault / file reference to broker credentials.
broker.secret DEPRECATEDstring—noInline secret; use secret_ref instead.
broker.failover_graceu3220noSeconds before falling over to next profile.
broker.client_idstringnode_idnoOverride client identifier sent to broker.
schema.field.<name>.typetype—yesPrimitive or composite type for a field.
schema.field.<name>.minnumber—noInclusive lower bound for numeric types.
schema.field.<name>.maxnumber—noInclusive upper bound for numeric types.
schema.field.<name>.monotonicboolfalsenoEach new value must exceed the previous.
schema.field.<name>.unitstring—noUCUM unit string for downstream display.
schema.legacy_json_compat DEPRECATEDboolfalsenoForces v1 JSON envelope; will be removed.
retention.raw_windowduration"24h"noHow long raw points are kept before rollup.
retention.aggregationslist[avg]noAggregations to compute: min, max, avg, sum, p50, p95, p99.
retention.export_targeturi—noOptional S3/GCS sink for expired rollups.
retention.export_schedulecron"@daily"noWhen to flush to the export target.

visibleText:
FerroFlux SDK v2.4 ⌕   Search documentation… GitHub Status Support Documentation Getting Started API Reference Configuration Changelog Troubleshooting Configuration Overview Global Settings Broker Profiles Device Schemas Retention Policies Key Reference Resources CLI Manual Rust crate docs Schema Registry Docs › SDK v2.4 › Configuration Configuration DSL FerroFlux nodes are configured through .flux files — a strict,
      strongly-typed configuration DSL that compiles to an immutable runtime
      manifest at boot. This page documents the four top-level blocks, shows
      annotated examples, and provides a searchable reference of all 47
      configuration keys. File location The daemon loads /etc/ferroflux/node.flux by default, then
      merges any files matching /etc/ferroflux/conf.d/*.flux in
      lexical order. Use ferroflux config check to validate before deploying. 1. Global Settings The global block defines node identity, logging behavior, and
      runtime limits. Exactly one global block is required per
      configuration tree; subsequent definitions raise E_DUP_GLOBAL at parse time. Identity fields ( node_id , region ) become immutable
      once the node has registered with a broker — changing them requires a full
      re-pairing. Most other keys hot-reload on SIGHUP . flux # /etc/ferroflux/node.flux — global section global { node_id = "edge-pdx-014" # unique within tenant region = "us-west-2" log_level = info # trace|debug|info|warn|error log_format = json metrics_port = 9100 workers = 8 # tokio runtime threads queue_depth = 4096 tls_min = "1.3" } 2. Broker Profiles A broker block declares a connection to an upstream FerroFlux
      Gateway (or compatible MQTT 5 / AMQP 1.0 endpoint). You may declare
      multiple profiles; the runtime fails over in declaration order when the
      primary becomes unreachable for more than failover_grace seconds. Credentials are referenced indirectly via the secret_ref field;
      the literal secret field DEPRECATED since v2.2 and emits a parse warning. It will be removed in v3.0. flux broker "primary" { endpoint = "tls://gateway.ferroflux.io:8883" protocol = mqtt5 tenant = "acme-industrial" secret_ref = "vault:kv/ferroflux/primary" keepalive_secs = 30 qos = 1 failover_grace = 12 } broker "fallback" { endpoint = "tls://gateway-eu.ferroflux.io:8883" protocol = mqtt5 tenant = "acme-industrial" secret_ref = "vault:kv/ferroflux/fallback" } 3. Device Schemas Device schemas describe the shape of telemetry payloads that a sensor
      class is expected to publish. The compiler turns each schema block into a zero-copy validator backed by serde + prost ;
      payloads that fail validation are dropped and surface as schema_violations_total in the metrics endpoint. Field types: f32 , f64 , i32 , i64 , u32 , u64 , bool , string(N) , enum(...) , and the composite vec3 for accelerometer triples. flux schema "thermal_sensor_v3" { version = 3 encoding = protobuf # protobuf|cbor|json field temp_c : f32 { min = -40.0 , max = 150.0 , required = true } field humidity : f32 { min = 0.0 , max = 100.0 } field battery_pct : u32 { max = 100 } field mode : enum ( "idle" , "active" , "calibrate" ) field ts : i64 { required = true , monotonic = true }
} 4. Retention Policies Retention policies bind to streams (matched by glob) and control how long
      raw and downsampled data remain on disk before being rolled up or evicted.
      A node may define any number of policies; the first matching glob wins. Caution Shrinking a retention window does not retroactively delete data;
      use ferroflux retention compact --since <ts> to force a sweep. flux retention "hot-thermal" { match = "streams/thermal/*" raw_window = "7d" rollup_window = "90d" rollup_interval = "5m" aggregations = [ min , max , avg , p95 ] storage_tier = ssd } retention "cold-vibration" { match = "streams/vibration/*" raw_window = "24h" rollup_window = "365d" rollup_interval = "1h" storage_tier = archive } Key Reference The table below enumerates all 47 configuration keys recognized by the
      v2.4 parser. Use the filters to narrow by block or required-status.
      Deprecated keys are flagged and will emit a warning at parse time. ⌕   Filter keys… All global broker schema retention required only Key Type Default Required Description global.node_id string — yes Unique node identifier within the tenant. global.region string — yes Geographic region tag, e.g. us-west-2 . global.log_level enum info no Logging verbosity: trace, debug, info, warn, error. global.log_format enum text no Either text or json . global.metrics_port u16 9100 no Port for the Prometheus exporter. global.workers u8 cpus no Tokio runtime worker thread count. global.queue_depth u32 2048 no Per-stream in-memory buffer depth. global.tls_min string "1.2" no Minimum negotiated TLS version. global.allow_plaintext DEPRECATED bool false no Allowed unencrypted broker links; removed in v3.0. global.shutdown_grace duration "15s" no Time given to drain queues at shutdown. global.heartbeat duration "30s" no Interval for self-health publication. broker.endpoint url — yes Broker URL with scheme and port. broker.protocol enum mqtt5 no mqtt5, mqtt311, or amqp10. broker.tenant string — yes Tenant slug provisioned on the gateway. broker.secret_ref uri — yes Vault / file reference to broker credentials. broker.secret DEPRECATED string — no Inline secret; use secret_ref instead. broker.keepalive_secs u32 60 no MQTT keepalive interval. broker.qos u8 1 no Publish QoS level (0–2). broker.failover_grace u32 20 no Seconds before falling over to next profile. broker.client_id string node_id no Override client identifier sent to broker. broker.persistent_session bool true no Use clean_start=false on reconnect. broker.max_inflight u32 128 no Unacked publishes allowed in flight. broker.compression enum zstd no none, gzip, or zstd. schema.version u32 — yes Schema generation; must monotonically increase. schema.encoding enum protobuf no Wire format: protobuf, cbor, json. schema.strict bool true no Reject payloads containing unknown fields. schema.field.<name>.type type — yes Primitive or composite type for a field. schema.field.<name>.required bool false no Whether the field must be present. schema.field.<name>.min number — no Inclusive lower bound for numeric types. schema.field.<name>.max number — no Inclusive upper bound for numeric types. schema.field.<name>.monotonic bool false no Each new value must exceed the previous. schema.field.<name>.unit string — no UCUM unit string for downstream display. schema.legacy_json_compat DEPRECATED bool false no Forces v1 JSON envelope; will be removed. schema.compat_mode enum backward no none, backward, forward, or full. retention.match glob — yes Stream selector glob, e.g. streams/*/raw . retention.raw_window duration "24h" no How long raw points are kept before rollup. retention.rollup_window duration "30d" no Lifetime of aggregated rollups. retention.rollup_interval duration "1m" no Aggregation bucket width. retention.aggregations list [avg] no Aggregations to compute: min, max, avg, sum, p50, p95, p99. retention.storage_tier enum ssd no ssd, hdd, or archive (S3-compatible). retention.compression enum zstd no On-disk codec for rollup chunks. retention.encrypt_at_rest bool true no Enable AES-256-GCM file encryption. retention.checksum enum crc32c no Per-chunk integrity algorithm. retention.priority u8 50 no Tie-breaker when multiple globs match. retention.evict_on_pressure bool false no Allow early eviction under disk pressure. retention.export_target uri — no Optional S3/GCS sink for expired rollups. retention.export_schedule cron "@daily" no When to flush to the export target. © 2025 FerroFlux Systems · Docs build 2.4.118-a91f Edit on GitHub · Report an issue · RSS On this page 1. Global Settings 2. Broker Profiles 3. Device Schemas 4. Retention Policies Key Reference See also Getting Started Troubleshooting v2.4 Changelog
